reason: Closes the splitSegments-quote-unaware bug (finding meta-260606T0301Z-...). The P1 G8 subcommand-class fix (meta-260606T0225Z-...) shipped a refined rule pattern but assumed splitSegments handled quoted message bodies correctly. Empirically 2026-06-06, a multi-paragraph git commit describing the P1 fix itself (with trigger phrases like 'propose/design/create a new schema, new schema, create schema' inside -m bodies) was still escalated by the rule, because the ';' in 'false positives; CLI subcommand' split the body into 16 segments, and each fragment was checked independently by the regex. The new state machine keeps the entire quoted body as one segment, so stripMessageFlags correctly strips the -m value and the regex sees only 'git commit'. Blast radius: every applyPromotedRules + matchConstraintPattern caller. POSIX shell semantics preserved (single quotes are literal; double quotes allow backslash escapes; backslash outside quotes escapes the next char).

reason: Links meta-260606T1500Z-closeout-script-idempotency-bug to meta-260608T0847Z-ttl-expire-system-has-the-wrong-action-for-expiry-current-se as a concrete case study of the TTL system's broken semantics. The TTL finding predicted that auto-resolve on expiry would make bugs disappear without trace; the closeout finding is the proof. This session (2026-06-09) only found the closeout bug because the operator explicitly asked to check it — the active set had already lost it to auto-resolve. The TTL finding itself shares the same fate: it was auto-resolved at 2026-06-09T02:10:37Z despite describing a still-active design flaw. Core insight: expired != resolved. A finding may be expired but the bug may still exist, or conversely the bug may be fixed but the finding was never properly resolved. The registry needs a stale-flag model (not auto-resolve) plus a meta_state_re_verify tool, per the TTL finding's own redesign proposal.

The fix lives in tool-registry.js#coerceParamsToSchema (the contract layer between wire format and Zod), adds a typeName-gated unwrapItemWrap helper (ZodArray or ZodObject only, max 3 iterations inlined). Zero changes to meta-state-patch-tool.js (the patch tool schema stays passthrough until Bridge 5). 4 stdio regression tests in __tests__/wire-format-patch-recursion.test.js (1 stdio combined-patch + 1 unit test on coerceParamsToSchema + 1 stdio propose_design + 1 pre-validation for empty arrays). reason: Phase B B2-1 + B2-2 + B2-3 shipped. The passthrough ZodObject on meta_state_patch's `patch` field is replaced with a per-kind union derived from metaStateEntrySchema's 4 per-kind branches via `buildPatchSchemaFor(kind)` (inlined in core/meta-state.js, NOT in a new core/schema-to-zod.js — that path was deleted in commit 05bea00 and stays deleted). Each per-kind schema is .partial() (patches are partial) and .strict() (rejects unknown keys and typo'd fields). Note: .strict() does NOT reject __proto__ via JSON.parse (JS engine absorbs it into prototype chain); explicit delete cleanPatch.__proto__ at core/meta-state.js:376 provides real defense. This eliminates the wire-format {item: [...]} quirk on top-level array fields (proposed_design_for, addresses) and removes the need for the 9 ad-hoc reader-patch sites. The registry unwrapItemWrap helper is preserved — it is tool-side coercion. meta_state_batch (line 483) is NOT covered; its .passthrough() is deferred. See plans/260613-1853-phase-b-bridge-5-core-fix for the full plan + phase files.

added:
**Closed loop and downstream enforcement:** the gap detector + session-start surfacing close the named un-block for the deferred SessionEnd/pre-commit hook. The detection surface is the set of bound-artifact prefixes whose edits should trigger a change-log (`CHANGE_LOG_BOUND_PATHS`); the surfacing reads the branch's touched paths via read-only `git` and joins them against change-log coverage derived from each entry's `change_target` + `applies_to.schemas` (canonicalized to absorb `#anchor` suffixes, the `learning-loop-mcp` → `learning-loop-mastra` rename, and bare loop-internal schemas). The result lands in `.claude/session-context.json` as `change_log_gap_hints` so the operator/agent sees "N bound edits on this branch have no change-log" and can backfill via `meta_state_log_change`.

The surfacing is advisory, not a gate — it cannot block edits or writes. Coarse prefix-descendant matching over-covers by design (a single `"docs/"` log silences all docs gaps); for an advisory signal, false-negative-safe beats noisy false positives. The detector intentionally leaves no persisted state; it derives the gap set per session and surfaces it. Recurrence ownership lives with the deferred SessionEnd/pre-commit hook, not here — that hook re-runs the detection at session end and keeps a per-(branch, path) gap counter so a *recurring* gap (drift rate above threshold) can be promoted into enforcement. The closed loop closes at the hook; this plan ships the detection the hook will call.

reason: Resolves meta-260717T1026Z-meta-state-patch-returns-patched-true-version-n-on-an-empty (gate-logic-bug, severity=warning, affected_system=meta-state-tools). meta_state_patch previously returned {patched: true, version: N} on empty patch objects — observed 2026-07-17 in debug session when the tool was called twice with `patch: {}` to attempt updating consolidated_into on a superseded finding. Root cause spanned four layers: (1) metaStateEntryPatchSchema = z.object({}).passthrough() at core/meta-state.js:633 accepted any shape; (2) per-kind `.partial().strict()` at line 618 made all fields optional; (3) meta-state-patch-tool.js handler had no empty-check before calling updateEntry; (4) updateEntry's entriesEqual no-op short-circuit at line 1128 (resolves meta-260715T2311Z-gratuitous-mutations) collapsed real-changes and no-ops into a single `true` return, making `patched: true` ambiguous. Defense-in-depth: schema refine rejects {} at parse time (catches direct core callers); handler-level empty-check fires BEFORE the CAS field is added, so the user sees the rejection for the patch they actually sent. Operator feedback loop is restored — typo'd patch paths / wrong tool choices no longer silently succeed. Public contract change: empty patches now return {patched: false, reason: "empty_patch"} instead of {patched: true, version: <unchanged>}. This is a strict superset of the previous behavior (real patches still work; legitimate no-ops via entriesEqual — e.g., setting category to its current value — are still short-circuited correctly, returning patched: true with no version bump). Out of scope: meta_state_batch update op has the same schema exposure via .passthrough() at batch-tool.js:23; the schema refine catches it for direct core callers, but the batch handler does not have a parallel empty-check. PR-body deltas: (c) new: meta-260717T1026Z; (e) superseded: meta-260717T1026Z → this entry.
